column6: A required unique identifier for the microsporidia strain. Must be unique across all species. Cannot be empty or 'NoMicrosporidia'.
Probability that an infected female transmits microsporidia to her eggs during oviposition.
Probability that a female mated with an infected male transmits microsporidia to her eggs, independent of her own infection status.
Probability that an infected female transmits microsporidia to an uninfected male during mating.
Probability that an infected male transmits microsporidia to an uninfected female during mating.
Multiplier for daily larval growth rate. Values greater than 1.0 accelerate development; values less than 1.0 slow development.
Multiplier for female adult mortality rate. Values greater than 1.0 increase mortality (shorter lifespan); values less than 1.0 decrease mortality.
Multiplier for male adult mortality rate. Values greater than 1.0 increase mortality (shorter lifespan); values less than 1.0 decrease mortality.

Time-dependent modifier for malaria acquisition probability. Uses an **InterpolatedValueMap** where **Times** represent days since microsporidia infection (ascending order) and **Values** represent probability multipliers (0-1). The model uses linear interpolation for times not explicitly defined; times beyond the maximum retain the last value.

Time-dependent modifier for malaria transmission probability. Uses an **InterpolatedValueMap** where **Times** represent days since microsporidia infection (ascending order) and **Values** represent probability multipliers (0-1). The model uses linear interpolation for times not explicitly defined; times beyond the maximum retain the last value.
